benchmark_unit: whole transcript -> final note quality score
purpose: Define the minimum reviewer training and anchoring requirements before clinician ratings are accepted into a validation evidence bundle.
learning_objectives: Score each final note against the whole consultation transcript rather than another note.
Apply the 0-1 score scale and ordered severity scale consistently across all candidate note sources.
Identify clinically significant omissions, unsupported hallucinations, medicolegal gaps, AHPRA concerns, PDQI-9 quality issues, and QNOTE domain gaps.
Preserve blinding by ignoring submission order and avoiding source or prompt-strategy inference.
Avoid direct patient, clinician, reviewer, provider, registration, phone, and email identifiers in reviewer comments.
Escalate ambiguous or disputed ratings through the adjudication workflow rather than changing the protocol ad hoc.

required_training_steps.evidence: reviewer attests that the scoring guide was read before scoring
reviewer attests that score bands and severity labels are understood
coordinator records completion outside the public repository
reviewer attests to blinding and no-identifier comment rules
pseudonymous reviewer registry has current registration, no conflict, and training_completed=yes

anchor_case_requirements.acceptance_rule: Reviewer may proceed to independent scoring after coordinator confirms anchor-case discussion and training attestation.

claim_boundary: Training completion improves reviewer consistency but is not itself validation evidence. Validation claims still require qualified independent clinician ratings, reviewer reliability, adjudicated consensus, and claim-readiness checks.
